evals.prompt: I'm about to submit a PR for the loqate-address-verification app version 1.0.1 in the address-verification domain. The ZIP is at address-verification/loqate-address-verification/loqate-address-verification-v1.0.1.zip. The root manifest has iconName set to 'loqate.png' but the ZIP actually contains icons/loqate-icon.png. Can you validate this app package?
evals.expected_output: Identifies icon filename mismatch - manifest says loqate.png but ZIP contains loqate-icon.png, explains why this will break (CI extracts by filename), marks validation as FAIL with specific fix recommendation to either rename icon file or update manifest

evals.prompt: Please validate the stripe-payments app located at payment/stripe-payments/stripe-payments-v2.0.0.zip before I submit the PR. The manifest entry has the correct iconName 'stripe.png', all translations are added, and the folder structure should be good.
evals.expected_output: Validates folder structure (payment/stripe-payments/ matches {domain}/{appName}/ pattern), checks icon filename matches manifest iconName, verifies translations in en-US.json, computes and validates SHA256 hash, confirms all requirements pass, marks validation as PASS

evals.prompt: I need to validate my tax app before submitting. It's located at tax/avalara-inc/avalara-tax-v1.0.2.zip. The app id in manifest.json is 'avalara-tax'. Is this ready to go?
evals.expected_output: Identifies folder structure violation - app is at tax/avalara-inc/ but should be at tax/avalara-tax/ to match app id, explains installation URL requirement, marks validation as FAIL with specific fix to move to correct location
